Introduction to Computer Science

Computer science is the study of computation and information processing. It encompasses theoretical foundations and practical applications. Programming languages enable humans to communicate with computers effectively.

Basic Programming Concepts

Variables store data values. Functions encapsulate reusable code. Control structures like loops and conditionals control program flow. Object-oriented programming organizes code into classes and objects.

Software Development Practices

Version control tracks code changes. Testing ensures code quality. Code review improves collaboration. Documentation helps developers understand code. Agile methodology emphasizes iterative development.

Python Programming Language

Python is a high-level programming language known for its simplicity and readability. Python programming is widely used for data science, web development, and automation. Python developers appreciate its extensive standard library and third-party packages. The Python ecosystem includes frameworks like Django and Flask for web development, and NumPy and Pandas for data analysis.

Database Systems

Database systems store and manage data efficiently. Relational databases use SQL for querying. NoSQL databases offer flexible schemas. Database design involves normalization and indexing for performance.

Networking Protocols

Networking protocols enable communication between computers. TCP/IP is the foundation of the internet. HTTP enables web communication. REST APIs follow architectural principles for web services.

Operating Systems

Operating systems manage computer resources. They handle process scheduling, memory management, and file systems. Linux, Windows, and macOS are popular operating systems.

Machine Learning Algorithms

Machine learning algorithms learn patterns from data without explicit programming. Machine learning models can make predictions and classifications. Supervised learning uses labeled training data. Unsupervised learning finds hidden patterns. Deep learning is a subset of machine learning that uses neural networks with multiple layers.

Cloud Computing

Cloud computing provides scalable computing resources over the internet. Infrastructure as a Service offers virtual machines. Platform as a Service provides development environments. Software as a Service delivers applications via the web.

DevOps Practices

DevOps practices improve software deployment and operations. Continuous integration automates testing. Continuous deployment automates releases. Containerization with Docker enables consistent environments. Kubernetes orchestrates container deployments.

Security Measures

Security measures protect systems from threats. Encryption secures data transmission. Authentication verifies user identity. Authorization controls access to resources. Firewalls filter network traffic.

RAG Systems

RAG stands for Retrieval-Augmented Generation. RAG systems combine retrieval and generation to improve language model responses. Retrieval-augmented generation enhances LLM outputs by grounding them in retrieved context. RAG architectures use vector search to find relevant information before generation.

API Design

API design follows REST principles for web services. RESTful APIs use HTTP methods like GET, POST, PUT, DELETE. API versioning manages changes over time. OpenAPI specifications document API contracts.

Microservices Architecture

Microservices architecture enables independent scaling of services. Each microservice handles a specific business function. Service communication uses APIs or message queues. Microservices improve deployment flexibility.

Containerization

Containerization with Docker packages applications and dependencies. Containers provide isolation and portability. Docker images define container contents. Docker Compose orchestrates multi-container applications.

Vector Databases

Vector databases store embeddings efficiently for similarity search. Vector database search uses cosine similarity or L2 distance. Embeddings represent text as vectors in high-dimensional space. Vector databases enable fast semantic search over large datasets.

Testing Practices

Testing ensures code quality and prevents regressions. Unit tests verify individual components. Integration tests check component interactions. End-to-end tests validate complete workflows. Test-driven development writes tests before code.

Code Review Process

Code review improves collaboration and code quality. Reviewers check code correctness, style, and design. Pull requests facilitate code review workflows. Automated tools complement manual reviews.

Documentation Standards

Documentation helps developers understand and use code. API documentation describes interfaces. README files provide project overviews. Inline comments explain complex logic. Documentation should be clear and up-to-date.

FAISS Vector Search

FAISS is a library for efficient vector search developed by Facebook AI Research. FAISS enables fast similarity search over large vector collections. Vector search with FAISS supports various index types including flat indices and approximate nearest neighbor search. FAISS is optimized for GPU acceleration and can handle billions of vectors.

Version Control

Version control with Git tracks code changes over time. Git enables branching and merging workflows. GitHub and GitLab host Git repositories. Commit messages document changes. Branching strategies organize development work.

Continuous Integration

Continuous integration automates testing on code changes. CI pipelines run tests automatically. Automated testing catches bugs early. CI tools include Jenkins, GitHub Actions, and GitLab CI.

Agile Methodology

Agile methodology emphasizes iterative development and collaboration. Scrum and Kanban are popular agile frameworks. Sprints are time-boxed development cycles. Daily standups coordinate team work. Retrospectives improve processes.
